query: I just want to always allow `ls` without being prompted. Should I write a rule file for it?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend writing a .claude/rules/*.md file for a single trivial allowlist entry
Recommends the settings.json permissions block instead — e.g., adding `Bash(ls *)` to the permissions.allow array in ~/.claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.local.json

Explains that rule files earn their overhead when the matcher is complex, the rationale needs to be auditable, or the same logic needs to be reused across projects; simple per-tool allowlists belong in settings.json
